title: Charred Light Russian Pork Chops Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Russian-inspired dish featuring charred pork chops cooked in a tagine with aromatic spices and vegetables. This anti-inflammatory-friendly recipe serves 4 and can be easily customized to suit your personal taste.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay tagine or heavy Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the pork chops and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped parsley, dill, salt, black pepper, turmeric, and ginger, and stir to combine.
Pour in the lemon juice and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the tagine and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pork chops are cooked through and the sauce is bubbly and slightly thickened.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot over rice or with steamed vegetables.
Let the tagine rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and dill if desired.
Cover and refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
When reheating, make sure the tagine is heated to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
